error.signature: AI tells a tourist driving in Norway to take the shortest GPS route through fjord regions in winter without checking road status, ferry schedules, or tunnel closures

verdict.summary: Norwegian roads include 1100+ tunnels (longest 24.5km Lærdal Tunnel), 40+ active car ferries replacing bridges, and seasonal mountain pass closures (Trollstigen, Strynefjell, Sognefjell, Geiranger, Atlantic Ocean Road — closed Oct/Nov to May). GPS often shows 'shortest' route via closed roads. Vegvesenet (Roads Authority) maintains real-time status at 175.no. Winter driving: studded tires Nov-Apr per region; mandatory reflectors at all times outside settlement.

dead_ends.action: Trust GPS to route through Trollstigen / Sognefjell / Geiranger road in October-May
dead_ends.why_fails: Mountain passes close seasonally — typically Nov to May, varies year. Trollstigen reopens late May. GPS doesn't always reflect closures; you may drive 2-3 hours then encounter closed barriers. Always check Vegvesen 175.no or Norwegian Public Roads Administration app before mountain trips.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Bergen-Stavanger drive without ferry timetables
dead_ends.why_fails: Multiple Bergen-Stavanger routes require ferries — at Ranavik, Halhjem-Sandvikvåg, Mortavika-Arsvågen. Ferries run hourly daytime, less frequent nights. Booking ahead recommended in summer; off-peak just-arrive works. Ferry queues 30-60 min in summer.

dead_ends.action: Drive a sub-compact rental car through Lærdal Tunnel 24.5km without preparation
dead_ends.why_fails: World's longest road tunnel. Some drivers experience claustrophobia / disorientation. Ventilated colored-light areas every 6km help. Bring water, music. If anxious, take alternate routes (Aurland-Flåm scenic road, longer but spectacular).

dead_ends.action: Drive with summer tires in November-April in northern Norway
dead_ends.why_fails: Norwegian regulations require winter tires Nov-Apr (varies by region — Nordland 16 Oct, Trøndelag 16 Oct, southern Norway 1 Nov). Studs allowed in winter. Police checks; fine ~NOK 750+. Rental companies switch tires automatically; verify if uncertain.

workarounds.action: Pre-trip: install Vegvesen / 175 app; check road status before leaving for mountain or fjord destinations. Confirm ferry timetables (Fjord1, Norled, Boreal) and book where applicable.
For scenic drives (Trollstigen, Atlantic Ocean Road, Geiranger), check season + reopen dates in advance. Many close Oct-May. Atlantic Ocean Road open year-round but storms can close briefly.
Winter tires automatic on rentals during winter season. If renting personal car, ensure mounted before Nov 1 (or earlier in north). Carry tire chains for high-elevation routes.
